unitTitle: Unit 2: Past simple, continuous & used to

content.subtitle: Use
items: Actions or events finished in the past
Things that happened again and again in the past
Situations that existed in the past but no longer do
The key events that move a story forward

content.subtitle: Examples
items: We **moved** to a new house last spring.
As a child, she **visited** her grandparents every Sunday.
People **didn't use** smartphones thirty years ago.
He **grabbed** his bag, **ran** down the stairs and **jumped** into the car.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: yesterday
last week/summer/year/etc
in January/2001/etc
an hour/a week/a year ago

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Some verbs have irregular past simple forms.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Describing what was happening at a specific time in the past
Two activities going on simultaneously in the past
Setting the scene at the start of a story

content.subtitle: Examples
items: At eight o'clock this morning, Dad **was making** breakfast.
She **was talking** on the phone while her brother **was playing** video games.
The sun **was shining** and the birds **were singing** — it was a perfect day.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: at that moment
at one/two/etc o'clock
while

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: When a shorter action interrupts a longer one in the past, combine the past simple and past continuous: I **dropped** my keys while I **was running** to the bus.
Do not use the past continuous for regular or repeated actions in the past.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Past habits or states that no longer exist

content.subtitle: Example
items: My grandfather **used to** walk five kilometres to school when he was young.

sections.title: A: Past simple (verbs in the box)
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past simple form of the verbs in the box. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: I was thirsty, so Dad ...... me a bottle of water.
We waited for Alex for an hour, but he ...... .
Sarah ...... a really interesting article for the school magazine.
Nobody ...... the answer to the teacher's question.
Tom ...... his wallet under the sofa yesterday.
My uncle ...... us to the airport early in the morning.
Last summer, we ...... some new friends at camp.
I was cold, so I ...... a new jacket.

sections.title: B: Match to make sentences (past simple)
sections.instructions: Match each sentence beginning (1–6) with the correct ending (A–F).

items.sentence: I don't want another sandwich because **A** I ate lunch an hour ago.
items.answer: A – I ate lunch an hour ago.

items.sentence: I don't need your bike because **B** I bought one last month.
items.answer: B – I bought one last month.

items.sentence: I know a lot about Rome because **C** I visited it last year.
items.answer: C – I visited it last year.

items.sentence: I don't have to clean my room now because **D** I tidied it yesterday.
I can't play that computer game any more because **E** I lost it last weekend.
items.answer: E – I lost it last weekend.

items.sentence: Dad is upset with me because **F** I forgot his birthday.

sections.title: C: Past continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past continuous form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: Mia ...... (read) a magazine at nine o'clock.
At midnight, we ...... (drive) home while it ...... (snow).
Ben ...... (wait) outside the cinema when his friends arrived.
I saw Olivia while she ...... (shop) in the town centre.
...... they ...... (watch) TV when the lights went out?
Ella ...... (cycle) to school when it started raining.
When you called Harry, ...... he ...... (sleep)?
At ten o'clock last night, we ...... (study) for the exam.

sections.title: D: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: When we were in Scotland, we **walked / were walking** in the mountains every day.
A few years ago, I **started / was starting** learning Japanese.
Emma **made / was making** dinner when the guests arrived.
We hurried home because it **snowed / was snowing** heavily.
Two children **fought / were fighting** in the playground, so a teacher stopped them.
Chris **sent / was sending** me a text message yesterday afternoon.
We **had / were having** dinner when somebody knocked at the door.
As I passed the kitchen, I saw Mum **baked / was baking** a cake.
I **slept / was sleeping** when the storm began.
While Jake **did / was doing** his homework, his sister listened to music.

sections.title: E: The School Trip
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past simple or past continuous form of the verbs in the box.
sections.passage:
Last Friday, our class (1) ...... very excited because we were going on a school trip. Some students (2) ...... their bags while others (3) ...... outside the school gate. It (4) ...... lightly, but nobody cared.
At eight o'clock, the bus finally (5) ...... . Everyone (6) ...... onto the bus quickly and the teachers (7) ...... hello to all the students.
While we (8) ...... to the museum, some people (9) ...... games and others (10) ...... loudly at jokes.
When we finally arrived, we (11) ...... around the museum in groups. Suddenly, one boy (12) ...... over while he (13) ...... a sandwich.
Luckily, he wasn't hurt and everyone (14) ...... laughing again a few minutes later.

sections.title: E: used to
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of used to. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: When I was a child, I ...... play outside for hours.
...... there ...... be a cinema in this street?
Sophie works in a hospital now, but she ...... want to be an artist.
I ...... enjoy spicy food, but now I love it.
...... your grandfather ...... wear glasses?
We ...... go camping very often, but now we do every summer.
